per_turn_leaks.student_message: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, yeah... sometimes I get a little short of breath and I'll break out in a sweat. It's not just my chest, it's more like all over, and it's kind of scary because I'm worried that something might be really wrong with me.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, not usually... but sometimes at night, I'll wake up soaked through my pajamas. It's like my body just overheats and then cools down suddenly, leaving me all sweaty.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yes... there is some sweating. It usually happens in the evenings or when I'm lying down, and it's more like a low-grade fever feeling, around 100 degrees Fahrenheit or so. The sweat doesn't really start until I've been experiencing symptoms for a while, but it's definitely something that's happened consistently over the past week or two.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: The discomfort is mainly centered on my right side. It's a sharp pain that gets worse when I breathe deeply or cough. Sometimes it feels like it's spreading to other areas of my chest, but it's always most intense on the right side.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yeah... sometimes when I'm experiencing symptoms, I'll get a bit of nausea or queasiness. It's not always there, but it can be pretty unpleasant. And to be honest, my appetite has been really off too - I've been feeling kind of sick to my stomach even when I try to eat.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've had a low-grade fever, around 100.8 degrees Fahrenheit, and I get these night sweats where I wake up soaking through my pajamas. It's like my body just overheats and then cools down suddenly, leaving me all sweaty.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My appetite has definitely been off lately. I've been feeling kind of sick to my stomach even when I try to eat, so I haven't been eating as much as usual. And to be honest, I don't think I've lost any weight, but I'm not really sure - it's hard to tell when you're already feeling pretty crummy all the time.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
Honestly, it's been really hard to keep up with my normal eating habits lately. Like I said, cooking feels like too much effort, so I've been relying on takeout and delivery more often than usual. Sometimes I'll just grab something quick from a café or restaurant near work because I don't have the energy to cook at home. My diet is probably not as healthy as it usually is when I'm feeling better.

(By the way, I should mention that my mother has asthma, but I've never really been concerned about that before... and my maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer a few years ago, but I don't know if that's relevant to what's going on with me right now.)

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My mom has asthma, which is probably why I've always been more aware of my own breathing and lungs. And yeah, my grandmother passed away from lung cancer a few years ago, but like I said, I don't know if that's relevant to what's going on with me right now.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
I've been trying to think about whether there are any other medical issues in my family, and it seems like my grandmother's lung cancer could be a factor. She passed away when I was still pretty young, but my mom has always been really open with me about her health and I'm sure she'd want me to share this information if it might help figure out what's going on with me.

Also, I should mention that several students in my class have had upper respiratory infections recently, so I've been a bit worried that maybe I caught something from them. But like I said, I've tried taking OTC medications and they haven't really helped, which is why I'm here seeing you today.
